name: MCP Notes
what_it_does: Lightweight MCP server for note-taking and note management. Enables AI agents to create, read, update, and organize notes — providing a simple local note store for capturing information, ideas, and context that agents can access and reference during conversations and workflows.
use_cases: Creating and managing notes from AI productivity agents
Storing research findings and summaries from information gathering agents
Maintaining context notes for ongoing agent workflows
Building lightweight knowledge capture systems with AI assistance
not_for: Teams using established note systems (Obsidian, Notion, Roam) — use those MCPs
Knowledge management requiring full-text search at scale
best_when: An individual wants a simple note MCP for AI agents to capture and retrieve information without the overhead of a full PKM system.
avoid_when: You use Obsidian, Notion, or another note system. Adding a separate note MCP creates duplication. Use your existing system's MCP instead.

auth.notes: Local note storage — no authentication required.

known_agent_gotchas: Notes stored locally — not accessible across machines or by other users
No full-text search in simple implementations — verify search capabilities
36 stars indicates modest adoption — evaluate maintenance before depending on
9Ninety org has limited public presence — assess longevity risk
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local note storage. No credentials. Note contents stay local. Low security risk.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local storage. No credentials. Low risk.
